rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant H & C Investment Associates, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment: hearing required to confirm that defendant has been dismissed, and if this is confirmed the court will take this motion off calendar. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Perini Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' strict liability cause of action is off calendar. As to defendant's duty argument based on foreseeability, defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated that the sophisticated use defense were applicable, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiffs' claims. While defendant's brief made arguments regarding substantial factor causation, defendant failed to properly put this issue before the court. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Rountree Plumbing Heatings Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Rountree Plumbing & Heating, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied. As to issue one (duty argument based on foreseeability), defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson. As to issue two, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated that the sophisticated use defense were applicable, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiffs' claims. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Amended) Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement failed to demonstrate that it propounded comprehensive contention discovery designed to elicit the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims against it and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Further, Mr. Cline's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it demonstrates that he worked with internal components original to Sarco equipment. Mr. Radle's declaration is insufficient to sustain defendant's burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
